tasks.title: Fix playthrough data isolation — ensure world truths never mutate from gameplay
tasks.description: CRITICAL: Audit and fix the PlaythroughOverlayStorage (server/db/playthrough-overlay-storage.ts) and client-side PlaythroughOverlay to ensure that ALL in-game mutations route through the delta/copy-on-write system and NEVER modify base world data. (1) Audit every PATCH/PUT endpoint in server/routes.ts that modifies characters, truths, items, quests, businesses, lots, residences, rules, actions — if the request includes a playthroughId, the mutation MUST go through PlaythroughOverlayStorage.interceptUpdate() rather than directly calling storage.updateCharacter(), storage.updateTruth(), etc. (2) Add middleware in server/middleware/ that detects when a request has a playthroughId header or query param and automatically routes writes through the overlay. (3) On the client side in DataSource.ts, ensure that all write operations (saveGameState, updateQuest, transferItem) pass playthroughId and use the overlay path. (4) Add integration tests that: create a playthrough, modify a truth via gameplay, verify the base world truth is unchanged, verify the playthrough delta contains the change, load a second playthrough and verify it sees the original truth. (5) Special attention to: truth mutations from Prolog rule execution, character stat changes from quest rewards, item transfers, reputation changes, NPC relationship changes. (6) Add a 'world mutation guard' that logs warnings if any storage.update*() call happens without going through the overlay when a playthrough context is active.

tasks.title: Implement comprehensive playthrough save data schema with all game subsystems
tasks.description: Expand the game save state structure in client/src/components/3DGame/WorldStateManager.ts to capture ALL relevant subsystem data for full game restoration. Current save captures player position/rotation/gold/health/energy/inventory but is missing critical data. (1) Add NPC schedule state: for each NPC, save their current schedule phase, current destination, isInsideBuilding flag, building they're in, time remaining in current phase, and position. (2) Add interior scene state: which building the player is currently inside (if any), interior layout seed for deterministic regeneration. (3) Add time system state: exact game hour/minute, ScheduleExecutor time scale, day number. (4) Add quest active state: currently tracked quest objectives with partial progress (e.g., '3/5 vocabulary words'), conversation quest turn counts, branching quest current branch. (5) Add language progress snapshot: vocabulary mastery levels, grammar accuracy, conversation count, CEFR progress. (6) Add reputation state: per-settlement standing, violation history, active bans. (7) Add NPC relationship deltas: relationship changes from this playthrough. (8) Add main quest chapter state: current chapter, per-objective progress. (9) Version the save schema (bump to v3) and add migration logic from v2→v3 in WorldStateManager.loadGameState(). (10) Ensure all subsystems implement getSerializableState() and restoreFromState() methods.

tasks.title: Fix NPC movement speed and eliminate teleportation artifacts
tasks.description: Address the issue of NPCs running too quickly or appearing to teleport. Root causes in NPCMovementController.ts and NPCScheduleSystem.ts: (1) Cap NPC walk speed to realistic values: stroll=1.5 m/s, walk=2.5 m/s (reduced from 3.0), hurry=4.0 m/s (reduced from 5.0). Adjust these constants in NPCMovementController.ts. (2) Fix the stuck-detection teleport in BabylonGame.ts updateSingleNPCBehavior() — when an NPC is stuck for 5+ ticks, instead of abandoning path and picking a random new goal (which causes visible teleport), smoothly slide the NPC 1 unit backward along their last movement vector, wait 2 seconds idle, then re-pathfind to the same destination. (3) Fix the pathfinding fallback in NPCScheduleSystem.ts: when A* finds no path, the current code returns [destination] directly causing instant teleport. Instead, find the nearest reachable sidewalk node and path there, then have the NPC idle for a schedule period. (4) Add interpolation smoothing: NPCs should lerp position between frames rather than snap to waypoint positions. Use Vector3.Lerp with a deltaTime-based factor in NPCMovementController.update(). (5) Add a maximum-distance-per-frame guard: if an NPC would move more than 0.5 units in a single frame (indicating a teleport), clamp the movement. (6) Fix NPCAnimationController speed scaling: ensure animation playback rate matches actual movement speed so walking animation doesn't play at 2x while NPC moves slowly. (7) When NPCs transition from overworld to interior (isInsideBuilding), fade them out over 0.5s rather than instantly hiding the mesh.

tasks.title: Implement NPC daily schedule variety with personality-driven randomization
tasks.description: Overhaul NPCScheduleSystem.ts to add variety and personality influence to NPC daily routines, replacing the rigid identical-for-all-NPCs schedule. (1) Make lunch timing vary: instead of all NPCs lunching at exactly 12:00, each NPC picks a random time in a 11:30-13:30 window, weighted by conscientiousness (high = earlier, low = later). (2) Make work start/end times vary: base is 9-5 but shift by ±1 hour based on personality. Early risers (high conscientiousness) start 8-9, night owls start 9-10. (3) Add weekend/rest day logic: NPCs don't work every day. Use a 7-day cycle where NPCs work 5-6 days based on work ethic. On rest days, they follow the unemployed schedule. (4) Add spontaneous activity injection: each schedule phase has a 15% chance (scaled by openness trait) to be replaced by a spontaneous activity — visiting a friend, going to a park bench, exploring a different part of town. (5) Add social interrupts: when two NPCs with high mutual relationship pass within 3 units, there's a 30% chance (scaled by extroversion) they stop and have an ambient conversation for 30-120 game seconds. (6) Add weather/time-of-year influence stubs for future expansion. (7) Store each NPC's generated daily schedule in a Map<npcId, DailySchedule> that regenerates each game day, not each frame.

tasks.title: Implement interior scene NPC entry/exit based on schedules
tasks.description: Make NPCs physically enter and exit buildings based on their schedules, visible to the player. Currently NPCs teleport/hide when entering buildings. (1) In NPCScheduleSystem.ts, when an NPC's goal destination is a building (business or residence), path them to the building door position first. (2) When the NPC arrives at the door (within 1.5 units), play a door-open animation/sound, fade the NPC mesh over 0.5s, and set isInsideBuilding=true with the buildingId. (3) When the NPC's schedule says to leave the building, reverse: fade in at door, play door-close sound, then path to next destination. (4) If the player is INSIDE the same building, the NPC should appear/disappear from the interior scene: call InteriorNPCManager.addNPCToInterior() when an NPC enters while player is inside, and InteriorNPCManager.removeNPCFromInterior() when they leave. (5) Track building occupancy: maintain a Map<buildingId, Set<npcId>> of who is currently inside each building. Expose this via a getBuildingOccupants(buildingId) method. (6) When the player enters a building, populate with all NPCs currently flagged as inside that building (not just the owner). (7) Cap building occupancy at a reasonable number based on building size (residence=4, small shop=6, tavern=12, church=20). (8) Show a subtle notification when an NPC enters/exits the building the player is in: '[NPC Name] has entered/left the [Building Name]'.

tasks.title: Implement business owner operational behaviors in interiors
tasks.description: Make business owners and employees actually perform their business functions when inside their businesses, not just stand idle. (1) Create a BusinessBehaviorSystem.ts in client/src/components/3DGame/ that defines per-business-type operational behaviors. (2) For each business type, define a set of work actions the owner/employees cycle through: BAKERY: knead dough (work animation at counter), check oven (walk to oven, work animation), arrange display (walk to shelf, work animation), serve customer (idle at counter, talk when player approaches). TAVERN: pour drinks (work at bar), wipe counter (work at bar), serve tables (walk to table, work), chat with patrons (talk at table). SHOP: arrange goods (work at shelf), count inventory (work at counter), assist customer (idle at counter, talk). RESTAURANT: cook food (work in kitchen), serve plates (walk to table, work), clean table (work at table). (3) Each action has a duration (15-60 game seconds) and an animation state (work, walk, idle, talk). (4) Business owners cycle through these actions during business hours. (5) When the player approaches the owner during a non-critical action (not mid-cook), the owner transitions to 'serve customer' mode and becomes interactable. (6) Add a 'business services' interaction: player can press G to initiate a business transaction (buy bread, order food, request service). (7) Wire business inventory from the world data so shops actually have items to sell.

tasks.title: Implement residence behaviors — sleeping, eating, relaxing at home
tasks.description: Make NPCs perform realistic home behaviors when at their residence. (1) Create a ResidenceBehaviorSystem.ts in client/src/components/3DGame/ that defines home activities based on time of day. (2) Night behavior (22:00-06:00): NPC goes to bed furniture, plays 'sleep' animation, stays until wake time (modified by personality per ScheduleExecutor). If player enters residence at night, NPC is in bed sleeping — approaching them shows 'Sleeping... [knock to wake]' prompt; knocking has a 50% chance of waking (grumpy response). (3) Morning behavior (06:00-08:00): NPC sits at table, plays 'eat' animation for breakfast (3-5 game minutes), then transitions to getting ready (idle at various furniture). (4) Evening behavior (18:00-20:00): NPC sits at table for dinner (eat animation), then relaxes — sits in chair (sit animation), or stands at window (idle animation). (5) When multiple NPCs share a residence (family members), they should be at the table together during meals and in separate beds at night. Use the residence's furniture layout from BuildingInteriorGenerator to assign beds/chairs. (6) If the player visits during meal time, the NPC offers hospitality dialogue: 'Would you like to join us for dinner?' — this could trigger a conversation quest objective. (7) Add ambient sounds: snoring at night, dishes clinking during meals.

tasks.title: Implement game time system with configurable timestep-to-realtime mapping
tasks.description: Create a unified GameTimeManager that synchronizes all time-dependent systems and maps to Insimul simulation timesteps. (1) Create client/src/components/3DGame/GameTimeManager.ts as the single source of truth for game time, replacing the scattered time tracking in ScheduleExecutor and BabylonGame. (2) Define the time model: 1 Insimul timestep = 1 game day. Each game day has 24 game hours. Default real-time ratio: 1 real minute = 1 game hour (so 24 real minutes = 1 full day/timestep). Make this configurable via world settings. (3) Expose: getCurrentGameHour(), getCurrentGameMinute(), getCurrentDay(), getCurrentTimestep(), getTimeOfDay() (dawn/morning/midday/afternoon/evening/night), getRealSecondsPerGameHour(). (4) Implement time-of-day lighting: dawn (5-7) warm orange, morning (7-10) bright, midday (10-14) full sun, afternoon (14-17) warm, evening (17-20) golden hour, night (20-5) dark blue with moonlight. Wire into BabylonGame scene lighting. (5) Implement a day/night cycle skybox transition. (6) Add a HUD clock display showing current game time and day number. (7) Support time pause (for menus, conversations, assessments) and time acceleration (2x, 4x for waiting). (8) Fire GameEventBus events at time boundaries: 'hour_changed', 'day_changed', 'timestep_advanced', 'time_of_day_changed'. (9) When a timestep advances, trigger server-side simulation tick via POST /api/worlds/:worldId/simulate-step if configured.

tasks.title: Wire VolitionSystem into NPC behavior loop for spontaneous actions
tasks.description: Connect the existing but unwired VolitionSystem.ts to the NPC behavior update loop in BabylonGame.ts so NPCs can spontaneously break from schedules to pursue autonomous goals. (1) In updateSingleNPCBehavior(), after checking if the NPC needs a new goal, call VolitionSystem.evaluateGoals(npc) to get a scored goal set. (2) If the top volition goal scores above a threshold (0.7), override the schedule-based goal with the volition goal. Set a 'volition_override' flag so the schedule system doesn't immediately reclaim the NPC. (3) Implement the three-level goal resolution: start goal (e.g., 'be_social') → non-terminal strategy (e.g., 'find_friend') → terminal action (e.g., 'walk_to_friend' → 'greet_friend' → 'chat_casually'). Each terminal action maps to a concrete movement target + animation. (4) When a volition action involves the player (e.g., 'greet_player', 'share_gossip'), trigger NPCConversationInitiator to approach the player. (5) Volition evaluation frequency: once per game hour per NPC (not every frame). Cache results. (6) Personality influence: extroversion boosts social goals, conscientiousness suppresses schedule-breaking, openness boosts curiosity/exploration goals, agreeableness boosts helping goals, neuroticism boosts conflict-avoidance. (7) After a volition action completes, NPC returns to schedule. Set a cooldown (2 game hours) before next volition override.

tasks.title: Implement NPC-to-NPC ambient social interactions
tasks.description: Enable NPCs to interact with each other spontaneously in the game world, creating a living town feel. (1) Create client/src/components/3DGame/NPCSocialInteractionSystem.ts. (2) Proximity trigger: when two NPCs are within 4 units and both are in 'idle' or 'walk' state (not working, sleeping, or in player conversation), evaluate whether they interact. Probability = (relationship_strength * 0.3) + (avg_extroversion * 0.2) + (0.1 if same workplace). (3) Interaction types: GREETING (2-5 seconds, wave animation, both stop briefly then continue), CHAT (15-45 game seconds, both face each other, 'talk' and 'listen' animations alternate, speech bubbles with '...' or generated snippets in target language), ARGUMENT (10-30 seconds, if relationship < -0.3, aggressive gestures), TRADE (5-15 seconds, if at marketplace). (4) Show floating speech bubbles above interacting NPCs with snippets in the target language (pull from a phrase bank per language). (5) Player can observe these interactions — if they approach within 3 units during a CHAT, they get an option: 'Join conversation [G]'. (6) Limit concurrent social interactions to 3 within player view radius to avoid performance issues. (7) Log social interactions to PlayTrace as 'npc_social_event' for analytics.

tasks.title: Create comprehensive main quest generator that produces a full game narrative
tasks.description: Overhaul server/services/main-quest-generator.ts to generate a substantive, world-specific main quest that serves as the backbone of an entire playthrough. Currently main-quest-chapters.ts has generic CEFR-gated chapter stubs. (1) The main quest should be generated per-world using the world's specific settlements, NPCs, businesses, languages, and cultural context. Use Gemini API to generate a narrative arc. (2) Structure: 6-8 chapters, each with 3-5 objectives, totaling 25-40 objectives for the full game. Estimated playtime: 4-8 hours. (3) Chapter themes should progress: Ch1 'Arrival & Orientation' (explore town, meet key NPCs, learn basic phrases), Ch2 'Building Connections' (help townsfolk, learn about local culture, establish relationships), Ch3 'Uncovering History' (discover town secrets through conversations, read historical documents in target language), Ch4 'Community Challenge' (a crisis that requires the player to use language skills to coordinate NPCs), Ch5 'Cultural Deep Dive' (participate in a festival/ceremony, demonstrate cultural competency), Ch6 'The Test' (a comprehensive challenge combining all learned skills), Ch7 'Resolution' (player's actions have changed the town, departure assessment). (4) Each objective should map to a completable in-game action using existing QuestCompletionEngine event types. (5) Objectives should reference specific NPCs and locations from the world data. (6) Generate quest-giver dialogue for each chapter's intro/outro using NPCs appropriate to the narrative. (7) Store the generated main quest as a quest chain with questChainId and proper ordering. (8) Add a 'Main Quest' section to the quest log UI (J key) that's always visible and distinct from side quests.

tasks.title: Implement main quest progress tracking with chapter unlocking and narrative beats
tasks.description: Build the runtime main quest progression system that manages chapter transitions, narrative cutscenes, and progress persistence. (1) Enhance server/services/main-quest-progression.ts: when a main quest objective completes, check if all objectives in the current chapter are done. If yes, trigger chapter completion. (2) Chapter completion flow: show a chapter completion screen with narrative text (outroNarrative), XP bonus award, summary of what the player accomplished, and a preview teaser for the next chapter. (3) Chapter unlock conditions: next chapter requires previous chapter complete AND minimum CEFR level (from main-quest-chapters.ts). If CEFR is too low, show 'Continue practicing to unlock Chapter N — requires [CEFR level]' with a progress bar toward the required level. (4) Store main quest state per-playthrough using PlaythroughOverlayStorage: currentChapterId, per-chapter completion status, per-objective progress. Never store on the world-level quest. (5) Add main quest status to the game HUD: 'Chapter 3: Uncovering History — 2/4 objectives complete'. (6) When loading a game save, restore main quest state from playthrough data and validate against current world state (in case world was edited). (7) Add a 'Main Quest Journal' UI accessible from the pause menu that shows the full narrative so far — all completed chapter summaries, key dialogue excerpts, and current objectives.

tasks.title: Implement auto-save system with event-driven triggers and save indicators
tasks.description: Harden the auto-save system in WorldStateManager.ts to reliably capture game state at critical moments. (1) Wire GameEventBus events to auto-save triggers. Critical events that MUST trigger a save: 'quest_completed', 'quest_failed', 'chapter_completed', 'assessment_completed', 'main_quest_objective_completed', 'player_death', 'settlement_entered', 'significant_purchase' (>50% of player gold). (2) Add periodic auto-save: every 5 real minutes during active gameplay (not paused). (3) Add a visible auto-save indicator in the HUD: spinning icon in corner with 'Saving...' text, fades after 2 seconds on success, shows red warning on failure. (4) Implement save deduplication: if two save triggers fire within 10 seconds, merge into one save. (5) Before writing save, validate state completeness: check that all SUBSYSTEM_KEYS have data, warn if any are null/undefined (indicates a subsystem didn't serialize). (6) On save failure (network error), queue to SaveQueue for retry and show persistent yellow indicator 'Unsaved changes'. (7) On browser close (beforeunload), use navigator.sendBeacon to fire a lightweight save with just player position, quest progress, and timestamp. (8) Add a save history: store last 3 auto-saves in separate slots (auto_0, auto_1, auto_2) rotating, so the player can recover from a bad auto-save. Manual saves remain in slots 0-2.

tasks.title: Build comprehensive PlaythroughAnalytics dashboard with detailed player journey data
tasks.description: Drastically improve client/src/components/PlaythroughAnalytics.tsx from its current minimal display (just status/actions/playtime) to a full research-grade analytics dashboard. (1) Add a 'Player Journey Timeline': chronological event feed showing all PlayTrace entries for a selected playthrough — quest starts, completions, failures, NPC conversations, location visits, item acquisitions, assessment results. Each event shows timestamp, type icon, description, and outcome. Paginated with infinite scroll. (2) Add 'Session Analysis': break playtime into individual sessions (detected by gaps >30 minutes in trace timestamps). Show per-session: duration, actions count, quests progressed, locations visited, NPCs talked to. (3) Add 'Quest Funnel': for each quest, show how many players started it, progressed each objective, completed it, failed it, abandoned it — as a funnel visualization. (4) Add 'Location Heatmap': table showing time spent in each settlement/building with heat coloring. (5) Add 'NPC Interaction Report': which NPCs the player talked to most, conversation turn counts, relationship progression. (6) Add 'Inventory & Economy': items acquired, items used, gold earned/spent over time. (7) Add filters: date range, playthrough status, player name search. (8) Add CSV export for all data tables. (9) Create the required API endpoint: GET /api/worlds/:worldId/analytics/playthroughs/:playthroughId/details that returns traces, sessions, quest funnels.

tasks.title: Build detailed Assessment Analytics with pre/post comparison and learning gains
tasks.description: Enhance client/src/components/AssessmentDashboard.tsx to provide clear research-grade assessment analysis. (1) Add 'Pre/Post Comparison' view: for each player who completed both arrival and departure assessments, show side-by-side scores per dimension (reading, writing, listening, conversation) with delta arrows (green up, red down). Calculate effect sizes (Cohen's d) across all players per dimension. (2) Add 'Learning Gains' chart: scatter plot of pre-test score (x) vs. post-test score (y) per player with a y=x reference line — points above the line = improvement. Color by CEFR level change. (3) Add 'Dimension Radar Chart': for a selected player, show a radar/spider chart with all assessment dimensions (vocabulary, grammar, pronunciation, listening, pragmatic, cultural) comparing pre vs. post. (4) Add 'Periodic Assessment Trends': line chart showing assessment scores over time for players who took multiple periodic assessments during gameplay. (5) Add 'CEFR Level Transitions': Sankey diagram or alluvial chart showing how many players moved between CEFR levels (A1→A2, A2→B1, etc.). (6) Add 'Subscale Deep Dive': for each instrument (SUS, SSQ, IPQ, ACTFL OPI), show subscale breakdown with norms comparison. (7) Add statistical summary: mean, median, SD, min, max per dimension with confidence intervals. (8) Create API endpoint: GET /api/assessment/analytics/:worldId that returns paired pre/post data, learning gains, and aggregated statistics.

tasks.title: Implement player action tracking for research-relevant behavioral analytics
tasks.description: Enhance the PlayTrace system to capture richer behavioral data useful for research analysis. (1) In BabylonGame.ts, emit PlayTrace events for ALL significant player actions, not just quest-related ones. Track: every NPC conversation start/end (with turn count and language used), every building entry/exit (with time inside), every item pickup/use/drop, every menu interaction, every map/quest-log open, every language switch, every vocabulary lookup, every time the player asks for help/hints. (2) Add 'engagement metrics' per trace: time_since_last_action (idle detection), actions_per_minute (burst vs. steady), repeated_action_count (grinding detection). (3) Add 'frustration indicators': rapid repeated failures on same quest objective, long idle periods, quest abandonment after multiple attempts, help requests frequency. (4) Add 'exploration metrics': unique locations visited, area coverage percentage, backtracking frequency. (5) Add 'social metrics': NPC conversation diversity (how many different NPCs), conversation depth (avg turns per NPC), relationship changes triggered. (6) Batch traces on the client and flush every 30 seconds via POST /api/playthroughs/:id/traces/batch (create this endpoint). (7) Store traces with efficient indexing: composite index on (playthroughId, timestamp) and (playthroughId, actionType).

tasks.title: Build Analytics overview page with cross-playthrough aggregated insights
tasks.description: Create a top-level Analytics overview that aggregates data across all playthroughs for a world, providing the world owner with research-grade insights. (1) Add a new 'Overview' tab to the analytics page that shows: total unique players, total play hours, average session length, completion rate (% of playthroughs that reached a 'completed' status), average CEFR improvement (pre→post delta). (2) Add 'Engagement Over Time' chart: line graph showing daily/weekly active players and total play sessions over time. (3) Add 'Quest Effectiveness Matrix': table of all quests showing completion rate, average time to complete, average attempts, and correlation with CEFR improvement (quests that players who improved most tended to complete). (4) Add 'Dropout Analysis': at which quest/chapter/hour do players most commonly abandon their playthrough? Show a survival curve (Kaplan-Meier style) of playthrough duration. (5) Add 'Player Segmentation': cluster players by behavior patterns (explorer vs. quest-focused, social vs. solo, fast learner vs. steady). Show segment sizes and characteristics. (6) Add 'Error Hot Spots': most-failed quest objectives, most-abandoned quests, longest time-to-complete objectives — these indicate design problems or learning bottlenecks. (7) Add 'Language Usage Statistics': most used vocabulary by players, most difficult words (highest error rate), most common grammar mistakes. (8) Add data export: 'Download Research Dataset' button that exports all playthrough data, traces, and assessments as a structured CSV/JSON bundle suitable for statistical analysis in R/SPSS/Python.

tasks.title: Ensure quest completability — validate all quest objectives against actual game mechanics
tasks.description: Create a runtime quest feasibility system that prevents uncompletable quests from being assigned. (1) In server/services/quest-feasibility-validator.ts (exists but needs hardening), add validation for EVERY objective type in shared/quest-objective-types.ts against what the game client can actually do. For each of the 40+ objective types, document which client-side system fulfills it. (2) visit_location/discover_location: verify the location exists in the world and is reachable via pathfinding. Check that the settlement/lot/building is generated in the 3D world. (3) talk_to_npc/complete_conversation: verify the NPC exists, has a schedule that puts them somewhere accessible, and the conversation system can reach them. (4) collect_item/deliver_item: verify the item exists in the world inventory or can be spawned, and the inventory system supports pickup. (5) use_vocabulary/collect_vocabulary: verify the vocabulary words exist in the world's language dictionary and the conversation system can detect their usage. (6) For objectives that require business interaction (order_food, haggle_price), verify a business of the right type exists and the business interaction system is wired. (7) Run this validator on all existing quests in a world via POST /api/worlds/:worldId/quests/validate-all — return a report of which quests are completable and which have issues. (8) Auto-run on quest creation and reject/warn about infeasible objectives.

tasks.title: Implement quest objective progress persistence across save/load cycles
tasks.description: Ensure partial quest objective progress survives game saves and loads. Currently QuestCompletionEngine tracks progress in memory but it may not fully serialize/deserialize. (1) In QuestCompletionEngine.ts, add getProgressSnapshot(): returns a Map<questId, { objectives: Map<objectiveId, { completed, currentCount, evidence[] }> }> capturing full engine state. (2) In WorldStateManager save flow, call getProgressSnapshot() and include it in saveData.questProgress. (3) In WorldStateManager load flow, call QuestCompletionEngine.restoreFromSnapshot(data) to rebuild objective states. (4) For conversation-based objectives: save the conversation turn count and any detected keywords/vocabulary so progress isn't lost if the player saves mid-conversation. (5) For location-based objectives: save which locations have been visited so re-visiting doesn't re-trigger. (6) For collection objectives: save the collected item IDs so duplicates don't count. (7) Add a test: start a quest, make partial progress (3/5 items), save, reload, verify progress shows 3/5. (8) Handle version mismatches: if a quest's objectives were modified in the world editor after the playthrough started, gracefully handle missing/changed objective IDs by preserving what matches and resetting what doesn't.

tasks.title: Implement playthrough-scoped reputation system with gameplay consequences
tasks.description: Wire the existing Reputation schema (shared/schema.ts) into active gameplay so player actions have reputation consequences, all scoped to the playthrough. (1) Create client/src/components/3DGame/ReputationManager.ts that tracks player reputation per settlement and faction. (2) Define reputation-affecting events: completing quests for a settlement (+rep), failing quests (-rep), helping NPCs (+rep with their faction), stealing items (large -rep), being polite in conversations (+small rep), being rude (-small rep). (3) Wire these into GameEventBus listeners. (4) Reputation levels affect gameplay: HOSTILE (<-50) = NPCs refuse to talk, guards may chase; UNFRIENDLY (-50 to -10) = higher shop prices, fewer quest offerings; NEUTRAL (-10 to 10) = default; FRIENDLY (10 to 50) = discounts, more quest options; REVERED (>50) = unique quests, special dialogue options. (5) Show reputation changes as floating text: '+5 Reputation with [Settlement]' in green/red. (6) Add reputation display to player stats panel. (7) Store all reputation changes as PlaythroughDeltas — never modify the base world. (8) Reputation affects which NPCs will offer the player quests and how NPCs greet the player in conversations (inject reputation context into NPC system prompts).

tasks.title: Implement playthrough metrics accumulation — playtime, actions, decisions tracking
tasks.description: The playthrough schema has playtime, actionsCount, and decisionsCount fields but they aren't being consistently incremented. (1) In WorldStateManager.ts or a new PlaythroughMetricsTracker.ts, implement a timer that accumulates real play seconds and periodically (every 60 seconds) sends a PATCH to /api/playthroughs/:id with the updated playtime. (2) Define what counts as an 'action': NPC conversation start, quest objective completion, item pickup, building entry, menu interaction, vocabulary examination. Increment actionsCount on each. (3) Define what counts as a 'decision': quest acceptance, quest abandonment, dialogue choice selection, branching quest choice, reputation-affecting action. Increment decisionsCount on each. (4) Add 'questsCompleted' and 'questsFailed' counters. (5) Add 'npcConversationsCount' and 'uniqueNPCsMetCount'. (6) Add 'buildingsVisitedCount' and 'uniqueBuildingsVisited'. (7) Add 'vocabularyLearnedCount' for total new words encountered. (8) Batch these updates: collect metrics locally and flush to server every 2 minutes to avoid excessive API calls. (9) On game exit, flush all pending metrics immediately. (10) These metrics feed directly into PlaythroughAnalytics dashboard.

tasks.title: Implement playthrough completion flow with summary and departure assessment trigger
tasks.description: Create a proper end-of-game flow when a player completes their playthrough. (1) Define completion conditions: main quest chapter 6+ completed OR player manually triggers 'End Playthrough' from pause menu. (2) On completion trigger, show a confirmation dialog: 'Are you sure you want to finish your journey? You can still continue exploring.' (3) If confirmed, trigger the departure assessment (if assessment-quest-bridge is wired). (4) After assessment (or if skipped), show a 'Journey Summary' screen: total playtime, quests completed/failed, NPCs met, vocabulary learned, CEFR level at start vs. now, favorite NPC (most conversations), most visited location, achievements earned. (5) Generate a 'Learning Report Card' comparing arrival and departure assessment scores per dimension with improvement percentages. (6) Update playthrough status to 'completed' and set completedAt timestamp via PATCH /api/playthroughs/:id. (7) Offer 'Continue Exploring' (status stays 'active'), 'Save & Exit' (status = 'completed'), or 'Start New Playthrough'. (8) Award completion XP bonus and any chain completion rewards. (9) Fire PlayTrace event 'playthrough_completed' with all summary metrics for analytics.

tasks.title: Add conversation and dialogue data to playthrough tracking for research
tasks.description: Capture detailed conversation data per playthrough for language learning research analysis. (1) Create a conversation trace system: for each NPC conversation, log: conversation start/end timestamp, NPC id and name, number of turns, language(s) used, player messages (anonymized or raw based on world privacy settings), NPC responses, vocabulary words detected in player speech, grammar patterns used correctly/incorrectly, CEFR-estimated complexity of player output. (2) Store conversation traces in PlayTrace with actionType='conversation' and rich actionData. (3) Don't store full conversation text in PlayTrace (too large) — instead store a conversation summary and a reference to the full transcript stored separately. (4) Add endpoint: GET /api/playthroughs/:id/conversations — returns all conversation summaries with pagination. (5) Add endpoint: GET /api/playthroughs/:id/conversations/:conversationId/transcript — returns full transcript. (6) In PlaythroughAnalytics, add a 'Conversations' tab: list of all conversations sortable by NPC, duration, turn count. Click to expand and see transcript. (7) Add conversation quality metrics: average turns per conversation, vocabulary density (unique target-language words / total words), grammar accuracy rate, code-switching frequency.

tasks.title: Implement NPC interaction system inside businesses — buying, selling, services
tasks.description: Enable actual business transactions between the player and business NPCs inside their establishments. (1) Create client/src/components/3DGame/BusinessTransactionSystem.ts. (2) When the player talks to a business owner (G key inside business), inject transaction context into the conversation: 'This NPC runs a [business type]. Available items/services: [list with prices].' (3) During conversation, detect purchase intent via keywords: 'buy', 'purchase', 'how much', target-language equivalents. (4) When a purchase is detected (via TRANSACTION marker in NPC response), show a transaction UI overlay: item name (in target language + translation), price, player's gold, confirm/cancel buttons. (5) On confirm: deduct gold from player inventory, add item to player inventory, update business stock, create PlayTrace event 'purchase'. (6) Implement selling: player can offer items to appropriate businesses (food to restaurants, materials to craftsmen). (7) Implement services: healer restores health, inn provides rest (fast-forward time), blacksmith repairs equipment. (8) All transactions in target language — the NPC describes items and states prices in the target language, player must demonstrate understanding. (9) Business stock is per-playthrough (via overlay) — if player buys all the bread, it's gone until restocked (next game day). (10) Wire 'complete_purchase' and 'sell_item' as quest objective types in QuestCompletionEngine.

tasks.title: Implement NPC greeting and ambient dialogue in target language
tasks.description: Make NPCs feel alive by having them greet the player and each other using the target language. (1) Create a GreetingSystem.ts that manages NPC verbal interactions. (2) When the player passes within 5 units of an NPC (not in conversation), the NPC has a 40% chance (scaled by extroversion and player reputation) to display a speech bubble with a greeting in the target language. Cooldown: 5 minutes per NPC. (3) Greetings vary by: time of day (good morning/afternoon/evening in target language), relationship level (stranger greeting vs. friend greeting), NPC personality (formal vs. casual). (4) Store a greeting phrase bank per language in the world data. Generate these during world creation using the language's vocabulary. (5) First-time greetings from new NPCs include their name: '[Greeting]! I am [Name], the [occupation].' (6) Player can respond by pressing G to start a full conversation, or just walk past (the NPC doesn't mind). (7) In interiors, the business owner always greets on entry with a business-appropriate greeting. (8) Show greetings as floating speech bubbles (Babylon.js GUI) above NPC heads that fade after 3 seconds. (9) Include subtitle/translation toggle: player can choose to see translation under the target-language text.

tasks.title: Implement save slot management UI with overwrite confirmation and slot preview
tasks.description: Build a proper save/load UI in the game's pause menu (GameMenuSystem.ts). (1) Save Screen: show 3 manual save slots + 3 auto-save slots (read-only). Each slot displays: screenshot thumbnail (capture via Babylon.js engine.createScreenshot), save date/time, playtime, current quest, player level, game day. Empty slots show 'Empty Slot'. (2) When selecting an occupied slot, show overwrite confirmation: 'Overwrite save from [date]? This cannot be undone.' (3) Save animation: brief freeze-frame + flash effect + 'Game Saved' notification. (4) Load Screen: same 6 slots with previews. Selecting a slot shows a detailed preview: player stats, active quests, current location, time played. 'Load' button with confirmation: 'Load this save? Unsaved progress will be lost.' (5) Add 'Delete Save' option (hold for 3 seconds to confirm). (6) Quick Save: bind to a key (F5) that saves to the most recently used manual slot (or slot 0 if none used). Quick Load: F9 loads most recent save. (7) Show last save time in the HUD corner: 'Last saved: 2 min ago'. (8) Handle save slot migration: if loading a save from a previous save schema version, show a compatibility warning.

tasks.title: Implement playthrough-scoped NPC relationship tracking with gameplay effects
tasks.description: Wire the PlaythroughRelationship schema into active gameplay so NPC relationships change based on player behavior, all scoped per-playthrough. (1) In DataSource.ts, load relationship overlays for the current playthrough on game start. (2) Track relationship changes from: conversations (positive turns increase affinity), quest completion for an NPC (+affinity), gift giving (+affinity), rude dialogue choices (-affinity), helping their enemies (-affinity). (3) Define relationship tiers: Stranger (0-20), Acquaintance (20-40), Friend (40-60), Close Friend (60-80), Best Friend (80-100). Negative: Disliked (-20 to -50), Enemy (-50 to -100). (4) Relationship level affects: NPC greeting warmth, willingness to help with quests, information they share, prices at their business (friends give discounts), whether they initiate conversations with the player. (5) Show relationship change notifications: '+10 Relationship with [NPC Name]' with a heart/broken-heart icon. (6) Add a 'Relationships' panel accessible from the pause menu showing all known NPCs and current relationship status. (7) Store changes as PlaythroughDeltas via the overlay system — base world NPC relationships are never modified. (8) Inject relationship context into NPC conversation system prompts so NPCs respond differently based on how well they know the player.

tasks.title: Add learning progress visualization to Analytics — skill trajectories and mastery curves
tasks.description: Create rich learning progress visualizations in the Analytics page for research insight. (1) Add 'Learning Trajectories' chart: line graph showing each player's CEFR-equivalent score over time (from periodic assessments and in-game performance metrics). X-axis = playtime or timestep, Y-axis = proficiency. One line per player with cohort average overlay. (2) Add 'Vocabulary Mastery Curve': chart showing cumulative vocabulary acquired over time per player, with retention curve (words that were correctly used again after initial learning). (3) Add 'Skill Dimension Radar': for each player, a radar chart with axes for vocabulary, grammar, pronunciation, listening, reading, writing, pragmatic, cultural — showing current mastery levels. (4) Add 'Quest-Learning Correlation': scatter plot showing quest completion rate vs. CEFR improvement — identifying which quest types correlate most with learning gains. (5) Add 'Conversation Analysis': word cloud of most-used target-language words across all players, most common errors, code-switching patterns. (6) Add 'Retention Analysis': for players who did delayed assessments, show retention curves by skill dimension. (7) All visualizations should use a charting library (recharts or chart.js already likely in project) with interactive tooltips and drill-down capability.

tasks.title: Implement playthrough delta garbage collection and performance optimization
tasks.description: Address the performance issue where long playthroughs accumulate thousands of deltas in PlaythroughOverlayStorage. (1) Add delta compaction: when a playthrough has more than 500 deltas, compact by merging sequential updates to the same entity into a single 'net delta' that represents the cumulative change. Keep the most recent fullData snapshot. (2) Add a compaction endpoint: POST /api/playthroughs/:id/compact-deltas that runs the compaction. (3) Auto-trigger compaction after every 100 new deltas. (4) For the in-memory delta index (Map<entityType, Map<entityId, DeltaEntry>>), add LRU eviction: keep only the most recent 1000 entries in memory, lazy-load older ones from DB on demand. (5) Add delta cleanup on playthrough deletion: ensure CASCADE delete removes all deltas, traces, and reputations (verify this works in mongo-storage). (6) Add a delta count metric to the playthrough record so analytics can show how 'active' a playthrough is. (7) Index deltas by (playthroughId, entityType, entityId) for efficient lookup. (8) Benchmark: measure load time for a playthrough with 100, 500, 1000, 5000 deltas and set acceptable thresholds.

tasks.title: Implement world state snapshot versioning alerts for active playthroughs
tasks.description: Enhance the world snapshot versioning system (shared/world-snapshot-version.ts) to properly handle the case where a world creator edits the world while players have active playthroughs. (1) When a world mutation bumps the version, check if any active playthroughs exist. If yes, log a warning and queue a notification for the world owner listing affected playthroughs. (2) On playthrough load, if the playthrough is 'behind' (up to 50 versions), show a detailed compatibility report: 'The world has been updated since your last save. Changes: [list of entity types that changed].' Give options: 'Continue with your save (may have inconsistencies)' or 'Start fresh (new playthrough)'. (3) For 'incompatible' playthroughs (>50 versions behind), offer: 'Your save is too old. Export save data (for reference) and start new playthrough.' (4) Add a PATCH /api/worlds/:worldId/playthroughs/:id/rebase endpoint that attempts to merge the playthrough's deltas with the new world state — keep player progress but update base data. (5) In the world editor UI, show a warning banner when active playthroughs exist: 'N players are currently playing. Changes may affect their experience.' (6) Add version change log: store what changed per version bump so the compatibility report can be specific.

tasks.title: Implement research data export with anonymization and structured formats
tasks.description: Create a comprehensive data export system for research analysis. (1) Add endpoint: POST /api/worlds/:worldId/research-export that generates a downloadable research dataset. (2) Export includes: playthroughs.csv (one row per playthrough: id, anonymized_player_id, status, playtime, actions_count, quests_completed, cefr_start, cefr_end, cefr_delta), traces.csv (one row per action: anonymized_player_id, playthrough_id, timestamp, action_type, action_name, outcome, location, duration_ms), assessments.csv (one row per assessment: anonymized_player_id, type, phase, score, max_score, cefr_level, timestamp), quests.csv (one row per quest attempt: anonymized_player_id, quest_id, quest_type, difficulty, status, attempts, time_to_complete), conversations.csv (one row per conversation: anonymized_player_id, npc_id, turns, vocabulary_used, grammar_accuracy, language_used). (3) Anonymization: replace userId with a consistent hash (same user gets same anonymous ID across all tables). Strip any freeform text that might contain PII. (4) Include a codebook.md explaining each column, data types, and valid values. (5) Support format options: CSV (default), JSON, or Parquet. (6) Add date range filter and playthrough status filter. (7) Include aggregate statistics summary: descriptive stats per variable, correlation matrix for key metrics. (8) World owner only — require authentication and ownership check.

tasks.title: Implement offline save conflict resolution with merge dialog
tasks.description: Address the gap where SaveQueue drops saves after 10 retries with no user feedback. (1) When SaveQueue fails to flush after 3 retries, show a persistent yellow banner: 'Your progress has not been saved to the server. Playing offline — data will sync when connection is restored.' (2) When connection is restored (online event), before flushing queued saves, fetch the server's latest save state for the same slot. Compare timestamps. (3) If server save is newer (another session saved in between): show a merge dialog: 'A newer save exists on the server from [timestamp]. Keep server version / Keep local version / Cancel'. (4) If server save is older: flush local saves normally (local wins). (5) If no server save exists: flush normally. (6) Never silently drop save data. If max retries exceeded, persist the save data to IndexedDB permanently and surface it in the save/load UI: 'Unsynced save from [date] — tap to retry upload'. (7) Add a connection status indicator in the HUD: green dot = connected, yellow dot = degraded, red dot = offline. (8) Test scenario: player plays offline for 30 minutes, generates 6 queued saves, goes online — all 6 should compact and sync as one save.

tasks.title: Implement NPC pathfinding improvements for smooth navigation
tasks.description: Improve the sidewalk-based pathfinding in NPCScheduleSystem.ts to eliminate jerky movement and unreachable destination issues. (1) Increase sidewalk graph resolution: add intermediate nodes every 5 units along long street segments (currently only at segment endpoints). This prevents NPCs from taking wide detours. (2) Add off-sidewalk pathfinding for short distances: if the destination is within 10 units and line-of-sight is clear (no building collision), allow direct path instead of routing through the sidewalk graph. (3) Implement path smoothing: after A* returns a waypoint list, run a smoothing pass that removes unnecessary intermediate waypoints where direct line-of-sight exists between non-adjacent waypoints. (4) Add building avoidance: when computing the direct fallback path (no A* path found), raycast from NPC to destination. If a building is in the way, path around it by adding waypoints at building corners. (5) Fix crosswalk connections: ensure every intersection has proper crosswalk nodes connecting both sides of each street. Currently some intersections may lack connections, creating unreachable zones. (6) Add path caching: cache A* results for common origin-destination pairs (home→work, work→tavern). Invalidate when the street network changes. (7) Visualize paths in debug mode: draw green lines showing NPC planned paths, red dots at stuck points.

tasks.title: Add playthrough comparison view for multi-playthrough research analysis
tasks.description: Create a comparison view in PlaythroughAnalytics that allows side-by-side analysis of multiple playthroughs. (1) Add multi-select checkboxes to the playthrough list. Select 2-5 playthroughs to compare. (2) Comparison metrics table: rows for each metric (playtime, actions, quests completed, CEFR change, vocabulary learned, NPCs talked to), columns for each selected playthrough. Color-code best/worst per row. (3) Timeline overlay: on a shared time axis, show when each player completed key milestones (first quest, first chapter, departure assessment). (4) Quest completion Venn diagram: show which quests each player completed, highlighting shared completions and unique paths. (5) Conversation volume comparison: bar chart showing NPC conversation counts per player, grouped by NPC. (6) Assessment score comparison: grouped bar chart showing per-dimension scores for each player. (7) Use case: compare two study groups (control vs. experimental) or track the same player across multiple playthroughs. (8) Add a 'Cohort' feature: group playthroughs by a tag (e.g., 'Group A', 'Group B') and compare aggregate metrics between cohorts.

tasks.title: Implement dynamic quest difficulty adjustment based on player performance
tasks.description: Create an adaptive system that adjusts quest difficulty in real-time based on the player's demonstrated language proficiency and gameplay patterns. (1) Create server/services/adaptive-quest-tuner.ts. (2) Track player performance signals: quest completion rate (last 5 quests), average attempts per quest, time to complete vs. estimated time, vocabulary accuracy rate, grammar accuracy rate, conversation quality scores. (3) If player is struggling (completion rate <50% or avg attempts >2.5): lower next quest difficulty by one step, increase hint availability, add more scaffolding to conversation objectives, reduce required vocabulary count. (4) If player is excelling (completion rate >90% and avg attempts <1.5 and time < estimated): increase difficulty by one step, add bonus challenge objectives, introduce more complex vocabulary, reduce hints. (5) Notify the player subtly when difficulty adjusts: 'The townsfolk notice your improving skills and speak more freely with you.' (6) Log all difficulty adjustments as PlayTrace events for research analysis. (7) Add a 'Difficulty Mode' setting: 'Adaptive' (default), 'Fixed-Easy', 'Fixed-Medium', 'Fixed-Hard' — for research control groups that need consistent difficulty. (8) Store current adaptive difficulty level per-playthrough (not per-world).
